title: His Answer to ן¿½Her Letterן¿½
content:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Being asked by an intimate party,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Which the same I would term as a friend,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Though his health it were vain to call hearty,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Since the mind to deceit it might lend;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ For his arm it was broken quite recent,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And thereן¿½s something gone wrong with his lung,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Which is why it is proper and decent
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ I should write what he runs off his tongue.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ First, he says, Miss, heן¿½s read through your letter
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ To the end,ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ and ן¿½the end came too soon;ן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ That a ן¿½slight illness kept him your debtor,ן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ (Which for weeks he was wild as a loon);
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ That ן¿½his spirits are buoyant as yours is;ן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ That with you, Miss, he ן¿½challenges Fate,ן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ (Which the language that invalid uses
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ At times it were vain to relate).

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And he says ן¿½that the mountains are fairer
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ For once being held in your thought;ן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ That each rock ן¿½holds a wealth that is rarer
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Than ever by gold-seeker sought.ן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ (Which are words he would put in these pages,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ By a party not given to guile;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Though the claim not, at date, paying wages,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Might produce in the sinful a smile.)

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ He remembers the ball at the Ferry,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And the ride, and the gate, and the vow,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And the rose that you gave him,ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ that very
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Same rose he is ן¿½treasuring now.ן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ (Which his blanket heן¿½s kicked on his trunk, Miss,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And insists on his legs being free
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And his language to me from his bunk, Miss,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Is frequent and painful and free.)

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ He hopes you are wearing no willows,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ But are happy and gay all the while;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ That he knowsֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ (which this dodging of pillows
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Imparts but small ease to the style,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And the same you will pardon)ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ he knows, Miss,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ That, though parted by many a mile,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Yet, were he lying under the snows, Miss,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Theyן¿½d melt into tears at your smile.ן¿½

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And ן¿½youן¿½ll still think of him in your pleasures,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ In your brief twilight dreams of the past;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ In this green laurel spray that he treasures,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ It was plucked where your parting was last;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ In this specimen,ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ but a small trifle,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ It will do for a pin for your shawl.ן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ (Which, the truth not to wickedly stifle,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Was his last weekן¿½s ן¿½clean up,ן¿½ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ and his all.)

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Heן¿½s asleep, which the same might seem strange, Miss,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Were it not that I scorn to deny
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ That I raised his last dose, for a change, Miss,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ In view that his fever was high;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ But he lies there quite peaceful and pensive.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And now, my respects, Miss, to you;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Which my language, although comprehensive,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Might seem to be freedom, is true.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ For I have a small favor to ask you,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ As concerns a bull-pup, and the same,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ If the duty would not overtask you,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ You would please to procure for me, game;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And send per express to the Flat, Miss,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ For they say York is famed for the breed,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Which, though words of deceit may be that, Miss,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Iן¿½ll trust to your taste, Miss, indeed.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ P.S.ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Which this same interfering
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Into other folksן¿½ way I despise;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Yet if it so be I was hearing
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ That itן¿½s just empty pockets as lies
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Betwixt you and Joseph, it follers
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ That, having no family claims,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Hereן¿½s my pile, which itן¿½s six hundred dollars,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ As is yoURS, with respects,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ 
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ TRUTHFUL JAMES.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Bret Harte (Francis), titled "His Answer to ן¿½Her Letterן¿½", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
